method.definedness: Kummer digit/residue test: no prime p <= k divides C(n,k).
method.deficiency: For defined C(n,k), delta counts i with n-k+i dividing i*C(k,i).
method.candidate_generation: Exact divisor enumeration of i*C(k,i); k<=100 from Python backend, k=101..129 from compiled backend cross-checked against Python on small k and known witnesses.
method.bound: n in [2k, floor(k + 2^k*sqrt(k))], with the floor computed as isqrt(k*4^k).
method.large_binomial_policy: Never forms C(n,k); C(k,i) is represented by exact prime valuations or exact small-k binomial values only.

sources.els93: P. Erdos, C. B. Lacampagne, and J. L. Selfridge, Estimates of the least prime factor of a binomial coefficient, Math. Comp. 61 (1993), 215-224.
